financial_metrics.label: Strong Beat

revenue.label: Strong Growth
revenue.analysis: Consolidated revenue from operations grew 67% year-on-year to INR 20,243 crore in FY25 from INR 12,114 crore in FY24, driven by robust growth across all four key business segments [p.49]. Food delivery revenue grew 27% YoY to INR 8,080 crore in FY25 [p.49]. Quick commerce revenue grew 126% YoY to INR 5,206 crore [p.49].

ebitda.label: Margin Improvement
ebitda.analysis: Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA grew 3x year-on-year to INR 1,079 crore in FY25 [p.16]. The Adjusted EBITDA margin improved to -1% in FY25 compared to -4% in FY24, largely due to better sourcing and supply chain cost efficiencies [p.25].

profitability.label: Improved Profitability
profitability.analysis: Consolidated PAT improved to INR 527 crore in FY25 compared to INR 351 crore in FY24 [p.49]. The improvement in profitability was driven by a significant increase in revenue and better cost management [p.49].

cash_flow.label: Positive Cash Flow
cash_flow.analysis: Net cash generated from operating activities was positive, with a net increase in cash and cash equivalents of INR 357 crore in FY25 [p.194]. Cash and cash equivalents at the end of the year stood at INR 666 crore, up from INR 309 crore at the beginning of the year [p.194].

capex.label: Investment Intensity
capex.analysis: Capex was incurred for expanding the footprint of the quick commerce business and working capital investments in the Hyperpure and going-out businesses [p.16]. No specific figures for total capex were disclosed in this document.

debt.label: Low Leverage
debt.analysis: No specific data disclosed in this document.

working_capital.label: Efficient Management
working_capital.analysis: Trade payables were non-interest bearing and normally settled on 0-60 days terms [p.230]. The company managed its working capital efficiently, contributing to positive cash flow from operations [p.305].

five_year_summary.label: Not Available
five_year_summary.analysis: No specific data disclosed in this document.

guidance_targets.label: Positive Outlook
guidance_targets.analysis: The company expects NOV growth to remain meaningfully above 100% even in FY26, with a steady-state Adjusted EBITDA margin of 5-6% of NOV [p.22].

segment_performance.label: Steady Growth
segment_performance.analysis: Food delivery revenue grew 27% YoY to INR 8,080 crore in FY25 [p.49]. The segment achieved an Adjusted EBITDA of INR 1,505 crore, reflecting a YoY improvement [p.7].

segment_performance.label: Rapid Expansion
segment_performance.analysis: Quick commerce revenue grew 126% YoY to INR 5,206 crore [p.49]. Despite the rapid growth, the segment reported an Adjusted EBITDA loss of INR 292 crore [p.7].

segment_performance.label: Strong Growth
segment_performance.analysis: Going-out revenue grew 186% YoY to INR 737 crore [p.7]. The segment reported an Adjusted EBITDA loss of INR 38 crore [p.7].

segment_performance.label: Moderate Growth
segment_performance.analysis: B2B supplies revenue grew 95% YoY to INR 6,196 crore [p.7]. The segment reported an Adjusted EBITDA loss of INR 84 crore [p.7].

financial_metrics.qualitative_analysis: The company demonstrated strong revenue growth across all segments, particularly in quick commerce and going-out. Earnings quality improved with a significant reduction in EBITDA losses. Capital allocation focused on expanding the quick commerce footprint and enhancing working capital for Hyperpure and going-out businesses. The financial trajectory indicates a positive outlook with expectations of continued growth and margin improvement.
analyst_flags: Strong revenue growth across all segments
Continued investment in quick commerce expansion
financial_metrics.sentiment_narrative: Management expressed optimism about the company's performance, highlighting a 67% YoY growth in consolidated revenue to INR 20,243 crore in FY25 [p.49]. They noted that the Adjusted EBITDA grew 3x to INR 1,079 crore, driven by improvements in food delivery and reduced losses in quick commerce [p.16]. The company achieved a consolidated PAT of INR 527 crore, marking a significant improvement from INR 351 crore in FY24 [p.49]. Management emphasized the robust growth in quick commerce, with revenue increasing by 126% YoY to INR 5,206 crore [p.49]. They also highlighted the strong performance in the going-out segment, with revenue up 186% YoY to INR 737 crore [p.7]. The company remains focused on sustaining growth and improving margins, with expectations of NOV growth remaining above 100% in FY26 [p.22].
financial_metrics.title: Financial Metrics

risk_intelligence.label: Well Managed
risk_intelligence.board_oversight: The board oversees risk through the Audit and Risk Management Committee, which ensures effective governance practices [p.92].

risks.label: High Risk
risks.analysis: Eternal faces significant risks from macroeconomic fluctuations and geopolitical volatility, which can impact interest rates, inflation, and economic stability. These factors may alter consumer purchasing behavior, reduce demand, increase costs, and affect profitability and cash flows [p.92]. The company actively monitors these trends and adjusts its strategy accordingly, including modifying product offerings and reallocating resources [p.92]. However, the inherent unpredictability of geopolitical events remains a concern.

risks.label: High Risk
risks.analysis: Eternal is exposed to cybersecurity threats, including ransomware attacks and data breaches, which could lead to significant financial losses and reputational damage [p.90]. The company has implemented an information security framework and holds ISO 27001 certification to protect against unauthorized access [p.90]. Regular assessments and bug bounty programs are conducted to enhance security posture [p.90]. Despite these measures, the evolving nature of cyber threats remains a concern.

risks.label: Moderate Risk
risks.analysis: Climate change poses a significant risk to Eternal's operations, particularly in its food ordering and delivery business. The company has assessed physical climate risks and integrated these findings into its Enterprise Risk Management process [p.86]. Ignoring these risks or failing to comply with evolving regulations could lead to increased operational costs, supply chain disruptions, and reputational damage [p.86]. The increasing regulatory focus on climate change suggests a rising trend in this risk category.

risks.label: Moderate Risk
risks.analysis: Eternal's credit risk primarily arises from trade receivables, which are unsecured and derived from customers in India and the Middle East [p.362]. The company manages this risk through credit approvals, establishing credit limits, and monitoring customer creditworthiness [p.362]. While the risk is currently moderate, any deterioration in economic conditions could increase the likelihood of defaults.

risk_overview.analysis: The overall risk disclosure in the annual report is comprehensive, covering a range of macroeconomic, environmental, and cybersecurity risks. The risk management framework is established, with clear internal controls and board oversight. However, there is a notable gap in the disclosure of emerging risks related to rapid technological changes and potential disruptions in the quick commerce sector.

undisclosed_risks: Potential regulatory changes in digital marketplaces
emerging_risks: Increased regulatory scrutiny in digital sectors
risk_intelligence.sentiment_narrative: Eternal communicates its risk profile with a focus on proactive management and strategic adjustments to mitigate potential impacts. The company emphasizes its commitment to sustainability and cybersecurity, reflecting a forward-looking approach. However, the communication could be enhanced by addressing potential technological disruptions and regulatory changes more explicitly. Overall, the sentiment is cautiously optimistic, with an acknowledgment of the challenges posed by external factors.
risk_intelligence.title: Risk Intelligence

esg.label: Excellent

environmental.label: Excellent
environmental.analysis: Eternal Limited has maintained its Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions at zero for the second consecutive year by using verified carbon removal offsets and International Renewable Energy Certificates (IRECs) [p.35]. The company reported a significant increase in Scope 1 emissions due to fugitive emissions from refrigerant refilling activities, but these were offset to maintain net zero emissions [p.124]. Eternal aims for 100% EV-based deliveries by 2030, with a 10.53% reduction in last-mile delivery emissions per km since FY22 [p.125]. Waste management initiatives include replacing disposable cutlery with reusable options and promoting waste segregation at source [p.127]. The company has also launched a Plastic-Free Future Program to encourage sustainable packaging among restaurant partners [p.126]. The environmental data is assured by Deloitte Haskins & Sells LLP [p.126].

social.label: Good
social.analysis: Eternal Limited has implemented comprehensive policies to ensure a safe and healthy workplace, achieving a Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate (LTIFR) of 0 for workers and 0.26 for employees [p.107]. The company covers 100% of its permanent employees with health and accident insurance, and provides maternity and paternity benefits [p.102]. Eternal has conducted a Human Rights Assessment with no material gaps identified [p.89]. The company promotes an inclusive workplace with dedicated ERGs for women, queer, veteran employees, and parents [p.113]. Eternal's talent attraction and retention strategies include structured recruitment drives and collaborations with external organizations [p.88].

governance.label: Excellent
governance.analysis: Eternal Limited's governance framework includes a well-balanced Board with six directors, including three women independent directors [p.146]. The company has established several committees, such as the Audit Committee and Risk Management Committee, to ensure effective governance [p.151]. Eternal has a robust policy framework promoting ethical conduct and compliance, supported by a whistleblower mechanism [p.54]. The Nomination and Remuneration Committee oversees director appointments and remuneration, ensuring alignment with the NRC Policy [p.52]. The company's governance practices are reinforced by a Code of Conduct for employees and directors [p.146].

esg.sentiment_narrative: Eternal Limited demonstrates a strong commitment to ESG principles, with comprehensive disclosures across environmental, social, and governance pillars. The company has effectively maintained carbon neutrality for Scope 1 and 2 emissions through offsets and renewable energy certificates. Social initiatives focus on employee well-being and inclusivity, with robust safety measures and insurance coverage. Governance practices are well-structured, with a diverse board and clear policies promoting ethical conduct. The ESG data is independently assured, enhancing credibility. Overall, Eternal's ESG performance shows a positive trend, with clear targets and initiatives aligned with sustainability goals.
esg.title: ESG Analysis

capital_structure.label: Very Strong

debt_profile.label: Not Available
debt_profile.analysis: No specific data disclosed in this document regarding the gross debt composition, net debt, leverage ratios, or debt maturity profile.

liquidity.label: Strong Liquidity
liquidity.analysis: Cash and cash equivalents amounted to INR 666 crores, up from INR 309 crores as of March 31, 2024 [p.223]. Bank balances other than cash and cash equivalents were INR 2,948 crores [p.249]. The company also held investments in mutual funds worth INR 1,910 crores [p.249]. The principal sources of liquidity include cash, cash equivalents, and liquid mutual funds, with no perceived liquidity risk [p.363].

capital_allocation.label: Conservative Allocation
capital_allocation.analysis: The Board did not recommend any dividend during the financial year [p.50]. There was no proposal to transfer any amount to reserves [p.50]. Retained earnings include amounts transferred from the share-based payment reserve, which are not available for dividend distribution [p.339].

credit_ratings.label: Not Available
credit_ratings.analysis: No credit ratings were obtained during the financial year as the company did not undertake any debt instruments or fixed deposit programs [p.165].

capital_structure.qualitative_analysis: The company's capital structure appears to be conservative, with a strong liquidity position supported by significant cash reserves and investments in liquid mutual funds. The absence of dividend payouts and transfers to reserves suggests a focus on retaining earnings for future growth or stability. The lack of disclosed debt or credit ratings indicates a potentially low reliance on external debt financing, reducing refinancing risk.
capital_structure.sentiment_narrative: Management emphasizes a strong liquidity position with adequate cash reserves and investments in mutual funds, ensuring the company can meet its short, medium, and long-term funding requirements without significant risk. The decision not to distribute dividends or transfer funds to reserves reflects a cautious approach to capital allocation, prioritizing financial stability and potential reinvestment opportunities. The absence of credit ratings and debt instruments suggests a strategic focus on internal funding sources, minimizing exposure to external credit market fluctuations. Overall, the company appears committed to maintaining a robust financial foundation to support its long-term strategic objectives.
capital_structure.title: Capital Structure

management_discussion.label: Excellent

industry_context.analysis: Eternal faces a challenging macroeconomic environment characterized by fluctuations in global and local conditions, including geopolitical volatility, which can influence interest rates, inflation, and economic stability [p.57]. These factors may erode consumer confidence and shift purchasing behaviors, potentially leading to decreased demand and increased operational costs [p.57]. Eternal actively monitors these trends and adjusts its strategy accordingly, including launching or modifying product offerings based on economic indicators [p.92]. The company also diversifies its portfolio across multiple verticals to cushion the impact of macroeconomic fluctuations [p.92].

operational_performance.label: Strong Demand
operational_performance.analysis: Eternal experienced significant growth in its quick commerce segment, with orders increasing from 119 million in FY23 to 424 million in FY25 [p.28]. The average order value also rose from INR 541 in FY23 to INR 667 in FY25, indicating strong consumer demand [p.28]. The average monthly transacting customers more than tripled from 2.9 million in FY23 to 10.2 million in FY25 [p.28]. Despite the competitive intensity, the company improved its Adjusted EBITDA margin to -1.3% in FY25 from -3.7% in FY24, driven by increased revenue per order and cost efficiencies [p.22].

forward_guidance.label: Aspirational Targets
forward_guidance.analysis: Eternal expects NOV growth to remain meaningfully above 100% in FY26, driven by investments in marketing and store expansion [p.22]. The company aims to achieve a steady-state Adjusted EBITDA margin of 5-6% as a percentage of NOV [p.22]. Capital work-in-progress for projects less than one year old amounts to INR 51 crores, indicating ongoing investments in infrastructure [p.268]. Eternal's strategic focus includes expanding its quick commerce business and enhancing operational efficiencies to support future growth [p.16].

key_themes.analysis: Eternal is exposed to geopolitical risks that may lead to rising inflation and input costs, compressing margins [p.92]. The company actively monitors these risks and adjusts its strategy to mitigate potential impacts [p.92].

Eternal has launched several sustainability initiatives, including the Plastic-Free Future Program, to encourage sustainable packaging solutions among restaurant partners [p.126]. These efforts aim to reduce environmental impact and promote responsible business practices [p.127].

The company continues to invest in technology infrastructure to enhance its digital capabilities, including cloud infrastructure and software development [p.64]. These investments are crucial for maintaining a competitive edge in the rapidly evolving digital landscape [p.56].

Eternal is committed to enhancing stakeholder experience through innovation and new product development, ensuring services align with customer preferences [p.56]. This focus on customer-first marketing and educational campaigns supports long-term brand building [p.56].

The company achieved improvements in operational efficiencies, contributing to a better Adjusted EBITDA margin in FY25 [p.22]. This was largely driven by increased revenue per order and cost management strategies [p.19].

management_discussion.qualitative_analysis: The MD&A section provides a comprehensive overview of Eternal's strategic initiatives and operational performance. However, the disclosure quality is medium due to limited quantitative data on certain strategic initiatives and potential risks. The management effectively communicates its commitment to sustainability and digital transformation, but more detailed forward-looking statements could enhance investor confidence. The narrative is clear, but some areas lack depth in explaining the impact of external factors on financial performance.
management_discussion.sentiment_narrative: Eternal remains committed to enhancing stakeholder experience and driving long-term engagement through innovation and new product development [p.56]. The company actively monitors macroeconomic trends and adjusts its strategy to align with changing market conditions [p.92]. Despite geopolitical volatility, Eternal has strategically diversified its portfolio to cushion the impact of macroeconomic fluctuations [p.92]. The company achieved significant growth in its quick commerce segment, with orders and average order value increasing substantially [p.28]. Management expects NOV growth to remain above 100% in FY26, supported by ongoing investments in marketing and infrastructure [p.22]. Eternal's focus on sustainability and digital transformation underscores its commitment to responsible business practices and long-term value creation [p.126, p.64].
management_discussion.title: Management Discussion

strategy.label: Excellent

vision_and_pillars.analysis: Eternal's vision is to power India's changing lifestyles through various business segments, including food delivery, quick commerce, going-out experiences, and B2B supplies [p.1]. The company aims to redefine the future by enduring, evolving, and empowering its stakeholders [p.1]. Core strategic priorities include enhancing stakeholder experience, driving long-term engagement through innovation, and developing high-performing talent [p.56]. The company emphasizes a long-term horizon, focusing on sustainable growth and brand building [p.56].

organic_growth.scope: Capacity expansion and new product development
organic_growth.label: Aggressive Expansion
organic_growth.analysis: Eternal is expanding its store footprint and investing in customer acquisition to drive growth [p.20]. The company has increased marketing investments to accelerate new customer acquisition, aligning with its expansion strategy [p.22]. Eternal's quick commerce business continues to expand its assortment to address more customer use-cases, including urgent medical care services [p.40]. These initiatives are expected to result in NOV growth remaining above 100% in FY26 [p.22].

inorganic_growth.label: Strategic Acquisitions
inorganic_growth.analysis: Eternal completed the acquisition of Orbgen Technologies and Wasteland Entertainment, expanding its presence in the movies ticketing and events business [p.237]. The acquisitions were executed through a combination of secondary share purchases and primary infusion, amounting to INR 758 crores [p.237]. These acquisitions are expected to enhance Eternal's consolidated revenue and profit, contributing to its strategic growth objectives [p.238].

digital_technology.label: Tech-Driven Innovation
digital_technology.analysis: Eternal is a technology-first organization, leveraging AI, machine learning, and data science to drive innovation [p.61]. The company introduced Nugget, an AI-native customer support platform, which autonomously resolves up to 80% of customer grievances, improving customer satisfaction and reducing costs [p.61]. Eternal also launched a web-based Restaurant Services Hub to support restaurant partners with essential services, enhancing operational efficiency [p.61].

international.label: Selective Expansion
international.analysis: Eternal's international presence includes dining-out experiences in the UAE, alongside its extensive footprint in India [p.1]. The company focuses on expanding its quick commerce and food delivery services across 800+ cities in India, with no specific international expansion plans disclosed in this document [p.1].

competitive_positioning.analysis: Eternal claims a competitive edge through innovation, differentiated products, and effective marketing [p.56]. The company faces competitive threats from new entrants and existing competitors, which could impact pricing and customer acquisition costs [p.56]. Eternal's strategy includes fostering long-term partnerships and enhancing operational efficiencies to maintain its market position [p.56]. The company is committed to setting new benchmarks in service excellence and digital convenience [p.55].

capital_allocation.analysis: Eternal prioritizes maximizing shareholder value through effective capital management, with no material debt reported [p.257]. The company has not recommended any dividend for the financial year, focusing instead on reinvestment for growth [p.50]. Eternal's capital allocation strategy includes funding acquisitions and expanding its operational footprint to drive long-term growth [p.237].

strategy.qualitative_analysis: Eternal's strategy is well-articulated, with a clear vision and specific growth targets. The company demonstrates a strong commitment to innovation and stakeholder engagement, supported by strategic acquisitions and technology investments. However, competitive pressures and talent retention challenges pose potential risks to execution. Overall, the strategy appears credible and aligned with long-term growth objectives.
strategy.sentiment_narrative: Eternal is committed to powering India's changing lifestyles through a robust strategy focused on innovation and stakeholder engagement. The company is expanding its footprint aggressively, leveraging technology to enhance customer experiences and operational efficiencies. Strategic acquisitions in the entertainment sector further bolster its growth trajectory. Despite competitive pressures, Eternal remains focused on setting new benchmarks in service excellence and digital convenience. The company's capital allocation strategy underscores its commitment to maximizing shareholder value while driving sustainable growth.
strategy.title: Strategy Overview

investment_synthesis.label: Buy

thesis.analysis: Eternal Limited, formerly known as Zomato Limited, has demonstrated robust growth across its diversified business segments. The company reported a 67% year-over-year increase in revenue, reaching INR 20,243 crore, and a significant improvement in EBITDA by INR 595 crore [p.2]. Eternal's strategic diversification into quick commerce and B2B supplies has shown promising results, with quick commerce experiencing a 113% YoY growth in net order value [p.3]. The company's focus on innovation and customer-first strategies, such as the introduction of new features like group ordering and food on train, further strengthens its market position [p.5]. However, challenges such as increased competition and the need for continuous innovation remain [p.56]. Overall, Eternal's strong financial performance and strategic initiatives position it well for medium-term growth.

bull_case.analysis: Eternal's diversified business model across food delivery, quick commerce, and B2B supplies provides a strong foundation for growth. The quick commerce segment, in particular, has shown impressive growth with a 126% increase in adjusted revenue [p.3]. The company's commitment to innovation, as seen in initiatives like food rescue and scheduled orders, enhances customer engagement and operational efficiency [p.5]. Eternal's strategic investments in technology and data protection bolster its competitive edge and mitigate governance risks [p.91]. Additionally, the company's robust financial health, with a 3x growth in adjusted EBITDA to INR 1,079 crore, underscores its operational efficiency and profitability [p.2].

bear_case.analysis: Eternal faces significant risks from increased competition and market saturation, which could lead to pricing pressures and higher customer acquisition costs [p.56]. The company's reliance on a large workforce and the challenges in attracting and retaining top talent could impede innovation and operational efficiency [p.88]. Regulatory changes and evolving consumer preferences pose additional risks to sustained growth [p.92]. Furthermore, the discontinuation of services like Zomato Legends and Zomato Everyday highlights the challenges in achieving sustainable scale and profitability in certain segments [p.5]. These factors could impact Eternal's ability to maintain its growth trajectory.

catalysts.near_term: In the next 1-2 quarters, Eternal's performance will be influenced by the continued expansion of its quick commerce segment, which has shown strong growth potential. The company's ability to effectively integrate new features and services, such as food on train, will also be a key driver of customer engagement and revenue growth.
catalysts.medium_term: Over the next 6-24 months, Eternal's strategic focus on innovation and customer-first initiatives is expected to drive growth across its business segments. The expansion of its B2B supplies business and the enhancement of its technology infrastructure will be critical in maintaining competitive advantage and operational efficiency.
catalysts.long_term: In the long term, Eternal's commitment to diversification and innovation positions it well to capitalize on structural tailwinds in the food delivery and quick commerce markets. The company's investments in technology and data protection will support sustainable growth and resilience against market volatility.

investment_synthesis.analyst_conclusion: Eternal Limited has shown strong financial performance and strategic growth across its diversified business segments. The company's focus on innovation and customer engagement, coupled with its robust financial health, positions it well for medium-term growth. However, challenges such as increased competition and talent retention need to be addressed to sustain its growth trajectory. Overall, Eternal's strategic initiatives and market position make it a compelling investment opportunity with a medium-term horizon.
investment_synthesis.sentiment_narrative: Management remains optimistic about Eternal's growth prospects, emphasizing the company's commitment to innovation and customer-first strategies. The focus on enhancing stakeholder experience and driving long-term engagement through new product development is central to Eternal's mission [p.56]. Management acknowledges the challenges posed by increased competition and market dynamics but is confident in the company's ability to adapt and thrive [p.56]. The strategic diversification into high-growth business portfolios is seen as a key strength in reducing reliance on a single service line [p.56]. Eternal's proactive approach to risk management and investment in technology underscores its resilience and readiness to navigate future challenges [p.91].
investment_synthesis.title: Investment Synthesis
